자르반 4세

As the king’s only son, Prince Jarvan is heir apparent to the throne of Demacia. Raised to be a paragon of his nation’s greatest virtues, he is forced to balance the heavy expectations placed upon him with his own desire to prove himself on the battlefield. An exceptional warrior in his own right, Jarvan also inspires his troops with fearsome courage and selfless determination, raising his family’s colors high and revealing his true strength as a future leader of his people.

"Words may make a ruler, but only actions make history." — Jarvan IV

Background

[edit | edit source]

Early Life

[edit | edit source]

Childhood

[edit | edit source]

Jarvan IV was born the only son of King Jarvan III of Demacia and his queen, the Lady Catherine. Though his birth was meant to bring great joy to his father and kingdom, the day was also marked with tragedy as Jarvan's mother died in childbirth. Jarvan III grieved and swore to never take another wife, placing all of his hopes and dreams in the future of his infant son, now heir apparent to the throne of Demacia.[2]

As he grew up, Prince Jarvan received the finest education the kingdom had to offer, and was guarded every moment he spent in the royal court. He learned much about Demacian virtues, including charity, duty, and service to his people, which Jarvan III hoped would raise his son into a good and just leader.[2]

Battle of Kalstead

[edit | edit source]
“ "My son, my life’s joy. How happy I am that you are here to witness this moment. To see for yourself why we must uphold our virtues, so others may aspire to do the same."
— Jarvan III to Jarvan IV, What Once Sailed Free

When Jarvan was still a young boy, his father brought him to the front near the Battle of Kalstead, where the Dauntless Vanguard won a decisive victory against disorganized Noxian conscripts. Many of the Noxians were scattered and taken prisoner, but believing there had been enough bloodshed, Jarvan III offered mercy and freedom to the prisoners should they agree to leave Kalstead in peace.[3] King Jarvan met with each of them in his tent, the prince sitting by his side. When the last of the prisoners arrived, a Reckoner called "Viscero", the King asked him to share his story with them. The man introduced himself as Xin Zhao, an Ionian from Raikkon before the empire had captured him at sea and brought him to their capital. He had not come to Kalstead by choice, having been bought out from the arenas and sent out to die for the empire.[4]

As with the others, King Jarvan offered Xin Zhao enough gold for travel from Kalstead, but he refused, instead requesting he be allowed to join the king's personal guard as thanks for his mercy. When asked how he could prove himself capable, Xin Zhao swiftly defeated the guards present and revealed that he could have killed the King at any point during their conversation. The display frightened the young prince, but Xin Zhao immediately apologized, and King Jarvan was simply impressed. He decreed that Xin Zhao be allowed to return with them to the Great City, and took the opportunity to impart an important lesson to his son about mercy: that even outsiders could be inspired by Demacian virtues, and should be given a chance to join their kingdom.[4]

Military Training

[edit | edit source]

As Jarvan grew older, he continued his trajectory to become a capable leader. Xin Zhao, who was chosen as his father's seneschal, took a central role in the prince's education, sharing his knowledge of the history and politics of Valoran, as well as training him in the arts of war and philosophy.[2] Among his peers, Jarvan also became close friends with a squire named Garen of House Crownguard, a family which had defended the royal line for generations.[5] They relied on each other heavily during this time, with Jarvan looking up to Garen's bravery and determination, and Garen relying on the prince's abilities as a tactician.[2]

When he came of age, Jarvan's father awarded him command of the silverwing riders who protected the Demacian capital. Though he was not expected as prince to actually lead the Demacians into battle, Jarvan was determined to prove himself worthy of this title. Jarvan's great-grandfather, King Jarvan I, had been slain by a Noxian in the first clashes between their nations in the south. Jarvan vowed to answer this insult by contesting the expansion of Noxus on the eastern frontier.[2]

Death of Xin Zhao

[edit | edit source]

As Yunara was being pursued by monstrous stags just outside the forests of Demacia, Jarvan IV, along with the platoon of soldiers under his command, rode atop their silverwing raptors to rescue her, crashing into the earth and leaving a deep crater where the monsters had been. As he dismounted to greet the visitor, Yunara presented to him a spearhead of Demacian steel, which had belonged to his old mentor Xin Zhao. Before Jarvan could ask any questions, bells began to toll from the city.[6] Jarvan took Yunara with him on his silverwing and flew her to the royal palace. There, he announced her to his father, Jarvan III, and revealed that their Seneschal was now gone.[7]

Some time after the news had been delivered, Jarvan went to visit his friend Luxanna, Garen's younger sister, as a guest instructor in her lessons about the history of early Demacia. Jarvan taught her about the death of his great-grandfather Jarvan I at the battle of Hvardis, where he had been slain by Sion, the Hand of Noxus. On the topic of sacrifice, the two spoke about the late Seneschal and his upcoming funeral, and though Jarvan was still in grief, he said it helped to speak of his fond memories of the man who had helped raise him. Jarvan was also looking forward to Lux's choir performance with the Illuminators,[8] but would be unable to attend the funeral when Noxians attacked Demacian allies in the east, and Jarvan went to defend the front with the Lord High Marshal beyond the Argent Mountains.[9]

Defeat by the Noxians

[edit | edit source]
“ "I led us to disaster... Careless fool! Father... Uncle... I've failed you. I've failed our people."
— Jarvan IV, Winged Protector

From his outpost high up in the Argent Mountains, which he had established against the advice of his lieutenants, Jarvan observed and disrupted the Noxian supply lines.[2] On one clear day, his scouts delivered intelligence of a small Noxian band in the lands beyond the mountains, and believing it would be a swift victory, Jarvan led his riders to force an enemy retreat. However, the Noxians had laid a trap for the Demacians; hidden in the crags of the mountains was Noxian artillery, which devastated the riders and shot Jarvan's silverwing raptor Aegis out of the sky.[10] Jarvan was flung from his saddle and fell hundreds of feet into the forests below.[2]

Badly wounded, and having lost his beloved silverwing and many of his soldiers, Jarvan fled as far as he could from the Noxians until he collapsed in the forest, where all he could do was reflect on his own failure. As he waited for death to claim him, Jarvan caught a glimpse of what he believed was the Winged Protector. This woman, however, was actually Shyvana, a half-dragon who forced the Noxians to retreat after they tried to capture the unconscious prince.[10]

Stand at Castle Wrenwall

[edit | edit source]
Main article: Forged in Fury

After saving Jarvan's life, Shyvana carried him back to the Demacians at Wrenwall, all without knowing who he was. He judged her a kind and worthy companion during his recovery, and though the garrison regarded her appearance with suspicion, she was allowed inside after Jarvan gave her his family crest from the broken pauldron of his armor.[11] It was here that Shyvana learned Jarvan's identity as the prince and only heir to the throne. But the brief peace would not last as the Demacians detected a great beast on the horizon: an elemental dragon named Yvva. As the wounded Jarvan was organizing the defense, he caught Shyvana trying to flee the settlement and confronted her. Shyvana confessed that she was the daughter of Yvva, and since innocent lives were in danger, her only choice was to head back into the wilderness to draw the beast away from the Demacians. Jarvan refused to let her leave; Shyvana had saved his life, and to repay her kindness, he and the Demacians would stand with her and end the beast once and for all.[12][2]

Jarvan and Shyvana proved a deadly team during the battle. The power of her transformation allowed her take the skies, and his leadership of the garrison, its archers, and healers held the line to support her. In the end they were victorious, and as thanks, Jarvan offered Shyvana a place in his personal guard. They returned to the Great City of Demacia together and presented the dragon's skull to King Jarvan III in the Hall of Valor.[12] Jarvan's father was overjoyed to learn his son was alive, but the nobles of his court regarded Shyvana with suspicion. Even so, Jarvan made certain she would have a place in Demacia, along with those who fought alongside them at Wrenwall.[2]

Battle of Evenmoor

[edit | edit source]
Main article: Ivory, Ebony, Jasper

As a hostile swarm of creatures known as the argoth closed in on the village of Evenmoor, Jarvan met with his generals to discuss strategy for their defense. General Ibell wanted to fight the argoth head-on, but General Mieser argued against this; so long as the argoth queen was commanding the swarm, any battle against them without reinforcements would amount to suicide. This in mind, Mieser believed their only option was to surrender Evenmoor to the argoth until they had time to regroup. Jarvan refused to abandon the village, however, and came up with a different plan: he and his best warriors would lay an ambush within Evenmoor, and when the queen was in view, they would kill her at the swarm's center before fighting their way out. His generals would lead the second wave, and with their plan set, Jarvan made for Evenmoor to slay the argoth queen.[13]

Betrothal Ceremony

[edit | edit source]
“ "Every maiden in the kingdom wants to wed me. Alas, they are all... not her."
— Jarvan IV to Garen, Lux: Issue 2

As time had passed, and the King had grown older, Jarvan found himself pressured to start looking for potential brides and secure the line of succession. This, Jarvan felt, was part of his duty to the kingdom, the noble houses, and also his father. As the prince, there was no shortage of women who would want to be chosen as his queen. But what Jarvan truly wanted was to marry someone who cared for him as a person, and not just because he was the prince. The one[14] he truly loved remained out of reach.[15]

As he and Garen were training together one day, Jarvan lamented the position the Demacian court had put him in. Garen suggested that Jarvan needed to move on and offered a solution: he could marry Garen's sister Luxanna, whom he already knew and trusted as a friend. This would surely satisfy Jarvan's father and the noble houses, and Garen convinced him to go ahead with the arrangement. Lux, however, had not been consulted about this, and she refused to attend the betrothal ceremony.[15] Jarvan was left waiting, and the other Crownguards were furious that she never showed up, leading to an investigation of her whereabouts.[16]

Great Mage Rebellion

[edit | edit source]

Death of the King

[edit | edit source]
“ "My father was wrong about your kind. He thought he could make peace with you. And you murdered him."
— Jarvan IV to Sylas, Lux: Issue 4

On the day of his scheduled execution, the convicted mage Sylas narrowly escaped before destroying an entire square and freeing the other mages from the Demacian mage prison.[16] Many of them fled the Great City through the sewers, but those who wanted vengeance joined Sylas on his march to the Citadel of Dawn, where they planned to publicly humiliate Jarvan III for all they had suffered. Having lost control of several portions of the city, Jarvan IV met with his father and the advisors and began to discuss strategy to defeat Sylas and his insurrection. The King, however, blamed himself for the carnage, claiming that Sylas had only become a threat to Demacia because they had put him in chains.[17]

As Sylas closed in on the palace, Jarvan ordered the guards to bring his father to safety as he went to personally confront the insurrection. His battle with Sylas was tense as he declared that the Citadel, which had stood for centuries, would not crumble to a band of lowborn traitors. But Sylas had studied Demacian history and the old battles of the Winged Protectors. The petricite around them held the magic of the Veiled Lady herself, which he used to chain Jarvan to the ground and capture him.[17]

Even as Sylas made his way to capture the king, Jarvan IV insisted that the reinforcements would arrive soon and surround the palace gates. Sylas did not care, believing he had already suffered far worse than anything Jarvan's men could do to him. To both of their surprise, the King was found dead in his inner chamber, surrounded by the bodies of his guards. Jarvan IV cried in horror and rushed to his father's side. He believed Sylas and the other mages were responsible, as the actual killer[18] had escaped the inner chamber undetected. Sylas was equally confused by Jarvan III's death, as he had been planning to publicly humiliate the king before executing him. Since he could no longer do this, Sylas decided to humiliate Jarvan IV in his father's place.[17]

Outside the palace, Sylas arranged a mock trial and execution for the prince, where the rebels declared him and his father complicit in the oppression of mages. Jarvan insisted that these were lies and that his father was a good man, but his pleas were ignored. As his "final meal", Jarvan would be forced to eat a rat, as Sylas had for many years in the mageseeker prison. Lux arrived and rescued the prince from having to do this, wounding Sylas with an arrow to the shoulder and a dagger to the foot. Her intervention also bought enough time for Garen to fight his way through and confront Sylas, who fled the scene. Jarvan, now freed by Lux and defended by his guards, tried to pursue him, but Garen insisted that it was too dangerous; as the sole heir to the throne, the kingdom needed him alive to lead them.[19]

Shortly before his death, Jarvan III had written a letter that would have immediately halted the arrests of mages, in hopes that it would prevent the brewing conflict. This letter was never made public, however, and when Jarvan IV found it, he had it destroyed.[14] Jarvan III's funeral was held the day after Sylas's escape, where Jarvan IV vowed that as the new king, he would avenge his father and formally declared war against "the scourge of magic." The announcement preceded a massive expansion of the mageseekers and their arrests, driving Lux and a number of other mages out of the Great City.[19]

The Mageseeker

[edit | edit source]
“ "I, Jarvan Lightshield IV, decree that all Mageseekers are ordered to cease their operations immediately. The city known as Terbisia shall be a place of refuge for mages, governed by Luxanna Crownguard. As king, I promise that I will find a way to bring them safety in Demacia. Any noble who still supports the Mageseekers is henceforth my enemy. Look for me on the field of battle, if you dare."
— Jarvan IV's decree for Shyvana, The Mageseeker

Following the death of his father and the escape of Sylas, Jarvan IV led the hunt for the Unshackled, believing he was his father's killer. As he had not yet been officially coronated, Jarvan's seat on the Silver Council of Demacia remained vacant, and in the months following the assassination, he left most of the actual governance to Tianna, Eldred, and the other council members as he continued to fight the rebellion.[20]

Sylas, who had joined forces with the Rebels of Zeffira, managed to deal numerous blows against Demacia and the Mageseekers, recruiting prisoners and growing the rebellion in the settlements of Thornbarrow, Clarion Cove, Devineur, and Glyndemere. After an armory in Capelworth was destroyed, Jarvan IV met with Garen in the palace war room. Jarvan was furious with himself for letting two Demacian towns fall into rebel hands, but Garen reassured his king that he would overcome these struggles, as his father had many times before. Garen believed that all they needed to quash the rebellion was to take down Sylas. Jarvan thanked Garen for his confidence, and the sword-captain left to seek more intelligence on the rebels. Shortly after Garen left, Jarvan spoke with his half-dragon bodyguard Shyvana, who had been listening to their conversation. Shyvana believed Garen's strategy would fail, as demonizing Sylas would not bring peace to Demacia nor to Jarvan. As a magical creature, Shyvana also believed Jarvan was giving the mageseekers too much power. Jarvan still trusted the mageseekers, and tried to reassure Shyvana that no matter what they thought of her, she would always have a place at his side. Shyvana found little comfort in his words, stating that the kind of king Jarvan was becoming would not look good with a dragon standing beside him.[14]

After Sylas returned to the Great City and assassinated Eldred Crownguard, Jarvan IV confronted Sylas in a duel to avenge his father. Sylas denied having killed the king, but gladly accepted the duel for a chance to kill another member of the Demacian High Command. Seeing them from afar, Shyvana flew to Jarvan's aid, and the two of them did battle against the Unshackled. Once Sylas had bested Shyvana in her dragon form, Jarvan placed himself between the two of them to protect her. Sylas finally realized that Jarvan was in love with her and denounced him as a hypocrite for outlawing magic. Before he could land the killing blow on them both, Shyvana used the last of her strength to shield them with a barrier of flames, and Sylas was forced to retreat with the other rebels. Shyvana collapsed soon afterward, and Jarvan caught her before she hit the ground.[14]

Following the attack, Jarvan met with Garen and Commander Wisteria, the new Mageseeker leader after Eldred's death. Angry that Sylas escaped once again, Jarvan declared that he had thus far been too lenient and ordered Wisteria to arrest every last mage in the kingdom. Garen believed this was going too far, but Wisteria argued that imprisonment was not going far enough, instead suggesting that the mages should be executed in case they be freed and recruited by the rebellion. Jarvan believed this was extreme, but felt he had no choice now that war had gripped every corner of the kingdom and approved the request, on the condition that Shyvana or Lux would not be harmed. With the new decree in place, Shyvana told Jarvan she was leaving Demacia, as his decision had ensured she would never have a future there. Jarvan pleaded for her to stay, stating that he never wanted to be king and would be unable to rule without her. Shyvana offered that they could run away together to the mountains where they had lived once before, but Jarvan refused, feeling he could not abandon his duty to Demacia and that it was too late for him to change. Shyvana replied that "no transformation is forever" before flying off into the distance.[14]

Wisteria, claiming that Lux was harboring terrorist mages in Terbisia, used her newfound authority to begin an invasion of the city. Jarvan met with Garen, and hearing Lux was about to be killed, Jarvan decided that Shyvana had been right about the mageseekers. He confessed to Garen about his father's letter which would have halted the mageseekers' arrests, which he had destroyed after his death, and that he had been trying to be a strong king instead of a good one. On Garen's advice, Jarvan decided the mages of Demacia had to be protected, and that the mageseekers were the true threat to peace. He also ordered Garen to defend Lux in Terbisia and order Wisteria to stand down, and that if he saw Shyvana, he should tell her that "no transformation is forever."[14]

After Wisteria had been killed and Terbisia began to rebuild, Jarvan IV penned a new decree to replace the one he had destroyed months earlier, formally ending the mageseekers' operations in Demacia and challenging any noble who continued to support them to meet him on the battlefield. Jarvan also appointed Lux the governor of Terbisia, which was to be a refuge for Demacia's mages until he could bring them safety in the kingdom proper. Privately, Jarvan hoped this decree would help Shyvana to feel safe again to return to Demacia. Sylas, who had recently risen as the rebel leader, believed the concessions Jarvan had made were not enough, and that he would ultimately fail to keep his promise unless he abdicated. As a result, the rebellion would continue. Later, Jarvan, Garen, and Lux stood looking toward the horizon, seeing Shyvana flying back to Demacia.[14]

Appearance

[edit | edit source]

Jarvan IV was a tall man with fair skin and dark brown hair, which was medium length and slicked back, and light stubble across his face. While leaner than warriors like Garen, his form was strong and battle-hardened by his years on the field. In his formal wear, Jarvan would often wear clean ceremonial coat of the signature Demacian white and blue, black underneath, and gold forming the pauldrons, collar, boots, and belt, as well as the crown on his head. When going into battle, Jarvan would be seen wearing full-body armor of black with gold and silver plating, with long dark spikes on the pauldrons and gauntlets. Jarvan wore a golden helmet while armored, which fashioned to resemble a crown with the face open, golden spikes on the helm, and a blue gemstone above his forehead. In his hands, he carried Drakebane, a long silver lance adorned with similar gemstones and spikes across its length.

Personality

[edit | edit source]

From a young age, Jarvan IV was raised to be a paragon of Demacia's greatest virtues. As the King's only heir, he felt a deep sense of responsibility to his father, his people, and the nation's stability, and would fulfill the courtly duties expected of his station, even when it was not what he wanted. The kingdom's future weighed heavily on his shoulders, as did the many expectations placed upon him. Jarvan idolized his father and sought to prove himself worthy of his crown, and would sometimes make rash decisions by ignoring the advice of his lieutenants. Whenever he failed his kingdom, Jarvan would become angry with himself, believing he was too weak to protect the ones he loved.

Jarvan IV was a skilled warrior and had the courage and the tactical brilliance to lead his soldiers from the front lines. If his people were in danger, Jarvan would refuse to abandon them, even if it meant placing himself in harm's way. Honor and commitment to one's fellow soldiers was a core tenet of Demacia, and Jarvan made sure to fight in the service of and inspire those such as Garen, Shyvana, and others he stood alongside. Those who threatened the people Jarvan cared about would be met with swift and violent retribution, and if Jarvan himself was to blame, he would regret his actions and try to make amends.

Abilities

[edit | edit source]
  • Leadership: As the crown prince of Demacia, Jarvan IV was raised in the royal court at a time when the nobility feuded among itself, and saw his father's efforts to bring stability to the kingdom. He became well read in the history and politics of Valoran under the tutelage of Xin Zhao and royal educators. After his father's death, Jarvan rose to a position where he was in charge of all the kingdom's elite forces, including the king's Dauntless Vanguard, as well as all matters of diplomacy and domestic affairs.
    • Strategist: Even during his military training, Jarvan was relied upon by recruits like Garen for his tactical brilliance. He was awarded command of the capital's silverwing riders when he came of age, and he led numerous successful campaigns in the Demacian borderlands, including those against elemental dragons and the argoth.
  • Warrior: Jarvan IV was personally trained by Xin Zhao, a former Reckoner who earned a fearsome reputation in the arenas of Noxus. Jarvan was able to translate Xin Zhao's fighting techniques into his own, and grew into an exceptional warrior in his own right.
    • Drakebane: An extendable lance passed down the royal line of Demacia. Jarvan could use it in close combat, and also leap into the air and land heavily on the terrain to decimate his foes.
  • Silverwing Rider: Jarvan IV was skilled at riding silverwing raptors, allowing him to take to the skies and serve as airborne cavalry using his lance.[6]

Relations

[edit | edit source]

Jarvan Lightshield III

[edit | edit source]

Jarvan III was the father of Prince Jarvan, his only child and heir. He once described his son as his "life's joy", and would always try to pass the wisdom and values of the Demacian royal family onto him in hopes that he would one day become a good and just king.[4] Jarvan IV loved and admired his father and would frequently try to prove himself worthy of the crown, even without his permission, which led to him nearly being killed by Noxian forces.[2]

Years later, during the mage rebellion, King Jarvan's death sent the prince into a spiraling grief and rage. His attempts to avenge his father led the kingdom into a full-blown civil war against Sylas and his rebels, even though Jarvan III had spent his final days trying to stop the brewing conflict and halt the arrests of mages. The absence of his father's guidance led Jarvan down a dark path, but Shyvana eventually convinced Jarvan IV to honor his late father's wishes instead of trying to avenge him.[14]

Xin Zhao

[edit | edit source]

Xin Zhao was the devoted protector of Jarvan IV and his father, Jarvan III, being chosen as the king's seneschal some time after the Battle of Kalstead. Xin Zhao had been a beloved mentor to the prince since he was a young boy, training him in history and politics, as well as the myriad arts of war and the spiritual philosophies of his distant homeland in Ionia. Xin Zhao was a trusted advisor and friend to both Jarvans, and the prince considered him like family.

Sylas of Dregbourne

[edit | edit source]

Sylas is looking to overthrow and kill the Demacian elite, including Jarvan. Jarvan IV was almost executed by Sylas and now is chasing him, believing Sylas is behind his father's death, although the king was already dead by the time he was found.

Garen Crownguard

[edit | edit source]

Jarvan IV met Garen at a young age when they both joined the military, and as brothers-in-arms, they became close friends. Garen looked up to Jarvan's abilities as a tactician, and Jarvan looked up to Garen's raw determination as a soldier. The two made an excellent team, and Garen always knew growing up that as one of the Crownguard family, it would someday be his duty to defend the prince once he was crowned king.[5] When Jarvan and Garen were older, they frequently went sparring together in the royal palace. Although they were friends, Garen would speak formally with the prince in accordance with protocol, calling him "my liege" even though Jarvan preferred to be called by name. Garen also knew that Jarvan loved Shyvana, but believed the prince needed to move on from not being allowed to marry her.[15]

After Jarvan became king, Garen believed Jarvan was no longer himself as he grieved his father's assassination, and was disturbed by how the mageseekers were able to manipulate him. Garen disobeyed some of Jarvan's orders to protect Lux, but also felt a great deal of shame for lying to a man who was both his sworn lord and best friend.[20] Jarvan, unaware of this, claimed that Garen was a "true Demacian" for being able to put his country first.[19] Though he was intent on defeating Sylas and the rebellion, Jarvan had no intention of hurting Lux or Shyvana, so when they were threatened by the mageseekers, Jarvan was finally convinced that he had been wrong to trust them. In his office, he confessed everything to Garen; how he had destroyed his father's letter, and how he had been trying this whole time to be a strong king rather than a good one. Garen reassured Jarvan and remained loyal to him, and they resolved to defend the mages in Terbisia against the mageseekers.[14]

Shyvana

[edit | edit source]

Jarvan IV was saved by Shyvana after escaping and later helped her in slaying her mother and, out of gratitude, she earned a spot in the Demacian Elite Guard. Jarvan mentioned he loved someone that wasn't so different from the mages.[17] This was later revealed to be Shyvana, and it was her influence that convinced Jarvan to honor his late father's wishes and dissolve the mageseekers.[14]

Luxanna Crownguard

[edit | edit source]

Jarvan IV was arranged to marry Lux by her family to protect her from being persecuted for being a mage: as queen, she would have been too powerful to accuse. After she didn't show up for the betrothal ceremony, the matter was apparently dropped.

Tianna Crownguard

[edit | edit source]

Tianna Crownguard was a trusted advisor to King Jarvan III for decades, having led the Dauntless Vanguard since the Battle of Kalstead.[4] In the months after his father's death, Jarvan IV left much of the kingdom's governance to Tianna and the other members of the Silver Council as he led the hunt for Sylas, as he had not yet been officially crowned king.[20]

Read More

Game

Demacia Rising

By Ty Sheedlo, Mayura Jain, Max Folkman

Long before the white ivory towers and glorious cathedrals of Demacia, there were only empty fields and desperate refugees. Their home lost to the chaotic magic of the Rune Wars, these stranded souls found refuge west of Valoran, deep in the petricite forest where magic could not touch them. Delve deeper into the creation of the kingdom of justice with Demacia Rising.

Starring: Fiora, Galio, Garen, Jarvan IV, Kayle, Lux, Morgana, Poppy, Quinn, Sona, Tianna, Valor

Mentioned: Jarvan III, Xin Zhao

Edit • Image • Reference

Game

The Mageseeker

By Digital Sun

In the kingdom of Demacia, the Mageseekers hold the power to oppress magic-using citizens in the name of public order–by inducting and indoctrinating them, locking them away, or driving them into hiding. Play as Sylas, a spell-stealing mage who has just broken free of his unjust captivity at the Mageseekers’ hands. Wielding the chains that once bound you, you must liberate your homeland from tyranny, one Mageseeker at a time.

Starring: Aidan, Eldred, Garen, Gideon, Hesbeth, Jarvan IV, Kara, Killan, Leilani, Lux, Morgana, Nocturne, Rayn, Rukko, Shyvana, Sylas, Wisteria, Yops

Short Story

Garen: First Shield

By Anthony Reynolds Lenné

The King is dead. Demacia is mourning. And in the eyes of Garen Crownguard, it’s his fault. But can he rise to the challenge when Demacia needs him again? While on a peacetime expedition beyond the borders of Demacia, Garen, Quinn, and the Dauntless Vanguard uncover a plot that threatens to destroy long-standing alliances. As the knight-ranger Quinn tries to get word back to Demacia, Garen and his comrades make a desperate last stand. How long can they last, and at what cost?

Starring: Cithria, Garen, Merrek, Quinn, Tianna, Valor

Mentioned: Eldred, Jarvan III, Jarvan IV, Kayle, Kindred, LeBlanc, Lux, Morgana, Swain, Sylas

Trivia

[edit | edit source]
Jarvan IV, the Exemplar of Demacia
  • Before the update to Xin Zhao's story in 2026, Xin had served Demacia for at least two decades during the events of the Mage Rebellion, acording to both his biography and the Aftermath story. Assuming that Jarvan was around 6 years old when Xin started serving Demacia, this would make him around 26 during the Rebellion, and around 19 during Swain's coup.
    • Following the update to Xin's story, his bio still mentions that he served Demacia for decades, despite his death taking place not long after Swain's coup. This means Jarvan is at least around 26 years when Xin died. Though its unknown if this was intentional or an oversight.
  • Jarvan IV's spear, Drakebane, was crafted by a Demacian weaponsmith named Orlon, the same one who crafted Poppy's Hammer.
  • 7 King Jarvan III's flavour text in Legends of Runeterra implies LeBlanc may have been involved in Jarvan's capture by the Noxian army.
  • A theater play depicting Jarvan IV's coronation was shown at the Piltover opera house in season 2 of Arcane.[21] As revealed in Xin Zhao's champion update, King Jarvan III was still alive at the end of the series,[22] meaning this performance occurred some years before Jarvan IV's actual coronation.
Jarvan IV, the Exemplar of Demacia (Old Lore)
  • In the  old lore, Jarvan IV's blood was used by Swain (his nemesis) and Vladimir to resurrect Sion. How they would have gotten the Exemplar's blood was never explored. LeBlanc is theorized to have impersonated Jarvan IV in order to cause a war between Demacia and Noxus.

Change log

[edit | edit source]

The following change log is for the Character blurb found at the top of the article:

Character blurb
5 Feb 2019 (V9.3) Updated to the current version.
3 Dec 2017 (V7.23) Updated.
Prince Jarvan, scion of the Lightshield dynasty, is heir apparent to the throne of Demacia. Raised to be a paragon of his nation's greatest virtues, he is forced to balance the heavy expectations placed upon him with his own desire to fight on the front lines. Jarvan inspires his troops with his fearsome courage and selfless determination, raising his family's colors high and revealing his true strength as a future leader of his people.
24 Mar 2017 (V7.6) Added with Galio's champion update.
Prince Jarvan IV comes from a lineage of kings, and is favored to lead Demacia in the next era. Raised to be the paragon of Demacia’s greatest virtues, Jarvan IV often struggles with the heavy expectations placed upon him. On the battlefield, he inspires his troops with his fearsome courage and selfless determination, revealing his true strength as a leader of men.

For historical versions of other stories, please see each story's respective change log.

    League of Legends content
     Dragon Strike COOLDOWN: 10 / 9 / 8 / 7 / 6
    Active: Jarvan IV extends his lance in the target direction, dealing physical damage to enemies hit and  reducing their armor for a short time.
    If the lance connects with a deployed Demacian Standard, he will dash to its location, knocking up enemies he passes by.
    League of Legends content
     Cataclysm COOLDOWN: 120 / 105 / 90
    Active: Jarvan IV leaps Unstoppably to the target enemy champion's location, dealing physical damage to nearby enemies upon impact.
    He then erects a circle of terrain that lasts for a short time.
    Recast: Jarvan IV destroys the created terrain.
    League of Legends content
     Martial Cadence TARGET IMMUNITY: 6 / 5 / 4 / 3 (based on level)
    Innate: Jarvan IV's basic attacks deal bonus physical damage equal to a portion of the target's current health.
    This effect cannot occur on the same target more than once every few seconds.
    League of Legends content
     Demacian Standard COOLDOWN: 12 / 11.5 / 11 / 10.5 / 10
    Passive: Jarvan IV gains  bonus attack speed.
    Active: Jarvan IV plants a flag at the target location that deals magic damage to nearby enemies.
    The flag lingers for a period, emitting an aura that grants  bonus attack speed to Jarvan and allied champions.
    League of Legends content
     Hammer Shock COOLDOWN: 8 / 7 / 6 / 5 / 4
    Active: Poppy smashes the area in the target direction, dealing physical damage to enemies hit based on their maximum health.
    The impact creates a brief field that slows enemies within, then ruptures to deal the same physical damage again.
    Legends of Runeterra
    Legends of Runeterra (or LoR) is a card game developed by Riot Games (development name was Project Bacon).

    Platforms
    • Microsoft Windows, Android, iOS

    Arcane

    Arcane (titled onscreen as Arcane: League of Legends) is an animated action-adventure series created by Christian Linke and Alex Yee for Netflix. Produced by Fortiche under the supervision of Riot Games, it is set in the League of Legends Arcane universe, and focuses on several characters from the games, particularly Vi and Jinx. The series was first released in November 2021.

    Platform
    • Netflix (Worldwide), Tencent Video (China)
